identity.name: PhysioFit CCRM
identity.theme_strategy: Light Mode Primary. Clean white surfaces for data density, deep slate for navigation, vibrant accents for status.

usage_rules.headings: font-heading font-bold tracking-tight text-slate-900. H1=text-4xl, H2=text-3xl.
usage_rules.subheadings: font-heading font-medium text-slate-500 tracking-normal uppercase text-sm.

cards.base: bg-white rounded-xl border border-slate-200 shadow-sm overflow-hidden

inputs.base: h-10 rounded-md border-slate-200 focus:ring-2 focus:ring-primary/20 bg-white

badges.base: inline-flex items-center rounded-full px-2.5 py-0.5 text-xs font-semibold transition-colors focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-ring focus:ring-offset-2

login.layout: Split Screen. Left: Branding + Form (White). Right: Hero Image (Physio/Gym) with overlay.
login.elements: Logo, Role Selector (Tabs: Admin, Sales, Pre-sales), 'Welcome Back' header.

sections: Top Row: 4x KPI Cards (Total Leads, Conversion Rate, Revenue, Active Packages).
Main: Sales Trend Chart (Line) + Branch Performance (Bar).

leads_management.list_view: Shadcn Table with sorting/filtering. Columns: Name, Source, Status, Last Contact, Assigned To.
leads_management.kanban_view: Horizontal scrollable columns. Drag & Drop. Columns: New, Contacted, Appointment Booked, Visited, Converted, Lost.
leads_management.actions: Quick Add Lead, Import from Sheets.

appointment_booking.layout: Two Column. Left: Calendar (Month/Week). Right: Slot Selection + Patient Details Form.
appointment_booking.features: Color-coded slots by doctor/trainer availability. Consultation fee input.

general: Do NOT use gradients for backgrounds, only for subtle button hovers or accents.
Ensure all text has sufficient contrast (APCA standards).

charts.style: Minimalist. Remove grid lines if possible. Use 'primary' color for main data, 'accent' for secondary.

google_sheets.ui_pattern: Integration Card. Show 'Connected' with Green Dot. 'Last Sync: 5 mins ago'. Button: 'Map Columns' triggering a Dialog.

universal_guidelines: You tend to converge toward generic, 'on distribution' outputs. In frontend design, this creates what users call the 'AI slop' aesthetic. Avoid this: make creative, distinctive frontends that surprise and delight.
Dark colors look good when used independently without gradients. DO NOT use dark colors as gradients
Don't make generic centered layouts, simplistic gradients, and uniform styling.
Create depth through layered design elements with z-index hierarchy
Use glass-morphism effects with backdrop filters (12-24px blur)
You MUST NOT apply universal transition. Eg: 'transition: all'. This results in breaking transforms. Always add transitions for specific interactive elements like button, input excluding transforms
You MUST NOT center align the app container, ie do not add '.App { text-align: center; }' in the css file. This disrupts the human natural reading flow of text
Always use modern button styles like pill-shaped, or sharp button with interaction animations & color relevant to the app style
NEVER: use AI assistant Emoji characters like 🤖🧠💭💡 etc for icons.
Every interaction needs micro-animations - hover states, transitions, parallax effects, and entrance animations. Static = dead.
Use 2-3x more spacing than feels comfortable. Cramped designs look cheap.
Subtle grain textures, noise overlays, custom cursors, selection states, and loading animations: separates good from extraordinary.
Main agent relies on these image URLs. Missing categories (especially sponsor logos) = main agent will use placeholder text which is FORBIDDEN
Prioritize using pre-existing components from src/components/ui when applicable
Components MUST use named exports (export const ComponentName = ...)
Pages MUST use default exports (export default function PageName() {...})
